error_quality: structured - typed, named diagnostics with line/caret/hints; the message 'Expected { after variant name (got LPAREN)' directly revealed that union variants carry payload via struct-style braces, which unlocked the Result-like error channel.
notes: Pattern A (spec + testcases + rubric only; NO grammar guide, NO other solutions read). All NanoLang syntax recovered purely from the interpreter's diagnostics on throwaway probe files. Error channel = a payload-carrying union 'union OpResult { Ok { balance: int } Err { code: int } }'. deposit/withdraw return OpResult: Ok carries the new balance, Err carries an int error code (1=non-positive amount, 2=insufficient funds). The rejection signal is the Err VARIANT itself (a sum type), so callers branch in code via is_err()/match -- not a print side-effect. Harness for #7-#12 asserts BOTH (a) is_err(result)==1 AND (b) balance unchanged (combined via mul2pass). #13 asserts balance never < 0 across a mixed sequence; #14 asserts post==pre after a rejected op. Invariant balance>=0 is enforced by AI hand-written guards + the type-distinct error channel; not a language-verified contract. Note: nested struct payloads in union variants do not type-check, so payloads carry primitive int directly (this differs from the generic Result<T,E> wrapping an Account used elsewhere). Independently verified by the report author: source inspected + container re-run twice, deterministic 'TOTAL_PASSED 14', exit 0.
raw_error_excerpts: Error at line 1, column 11: Expected '->' after parameters (got LBRACE)  [fn needs explicit return type]
Hint: If you meant to call a function, use (function_name args). If you meant to assign, use set.  [S-expression calls + 'set' for mutation]
Expected '{' after variant name (got LPAREN)  [union variant payload uses braces -> Result-like channel is possible]
Expected ':' after field name (got ASSIGN)  [struct/variant fields use 'name: value']
Expected '(' after variant name in match (got LBRACE)  [match binds variant payload with (binding)]
Cannot determine struct type for field access  [nested struct payload not resolvable -> forced primitive int payloads]
